mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings and consumer credit expansion directly increase aggregate demand, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising savings and credit periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that compress wage growth and improve profit margins, triggering immediate positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold crossing, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized supply-side capacity expansion meeting robust demand, which compresses input cost inflation and lifts equity valuations over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a sustained monetary tightening cycle, rising short-term policy rates increase the discount rate applied to distant cash flows, systematically depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during consecutive 3-month rate hike periods, or the forward return correlation with rate changes flips sign.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing output and operational efficiency, which directly supports near-term corporate revenue growth and drives positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates a negative or zero information coefficient over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between rising and falling INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds the 2.5 percent annualized threshold, it confirms an overheating economy that forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy normalization, ultimately weighing on equity multiples and producing negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or show no statistical decline when GDP growth crosses the 2.5% threshold, or the predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in nonfarm payrolls and consumer credit balances indicates broad-based income growth fueling consumption, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive information ratio over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction effect becomes negative when labor and credit growth diverge.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of persistent low inflation, a sudden spike in the CPI index signals a regime shift toward higher price stability expectations, which reduces real interest rates and triggers immediate positive equity returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: Equity returns decline or show no statistical response when CPI spikes above the 2.0 percent annualized pace, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.
